reasoning: To trigger a YES resolution, Portugal would need to experience >10 times its 30-day average of ACLED-defined 'Riots' over a 30-day period. With the reference value sitting at 3.333, this implies a 30-day average historically of about 2.33 riots per month. To exceed 10 * 3.333, there must be at least 34 distinct riot events in a single month. This demands more riots in a 30-day span than typically occur in a full calendar year. While there is a scheduled early election on May 18, 2025 due to a political crisis, and minor clashes were noted on April 25, Portugal remains historically uninclined to waves of violent, property-destructive protests on the scale of >30 distinct recorded ACLED riot events per month (the sole exception being the reaction to Odair Moniz's death in October 2024). Given that we are already on April 27, the window for May 4 is almost closed with no ongoing widespread riots. The subsequent windows are similarly extremely unlikely to hit such a massive, specific event threshold. Probabilities reflect the near-floor likelihood, drifting upwards slightly towards October purely to represent increased time horizon uncertainty.
